auth.envMisconfigured: Authentication failed because required environment settings are missing or invalid. Check NEXTAUTH_SECRET and NEXTAUTH_URL.
auth.serviceUnavailable: Authentication is temporarily unavailable. Check database connectivity and deployment environment settings.

items.next: Locations CRUD is the next expansion point after the remaining shell foundation polish.

operationalReset.title: Operational data reset
operationalReset.description: Delete operational data from the live database before importing a fresh EST dataset.
operationalReset.warning: This action removes cycles, sessions, assignments, waiting-list entries, attendance, evaluations, blocks, locations, imported proctors, activity logs, and active app sessions.
operationalReset.preserve: App accounts, role definitions, runtime settings, and email templates are preserved so the system can be repopulated safely.
operationalReset.confirmLabel: Type RESET to enable the destructive action.
operationalReset.previewFailed: Could not load the current data footprint.
operationalReset.success: Operational data was cleared successfully. Sign in again if your current session was removed.
operationalReset.forbidden: Only super admins can reset operational data.

cycles.title: Cycles management
cycles.subtitle: Create, monitor, and maintain exam cycles from the live cycle API.

cycles.deleteHelp: Deactivate keeps the record in the database and hides it from active workflows. Permanent delete removes it completely and is available only when no related sessions, waiting-list entries, or cloned cycles exist.

form.subtitle: Cycle start and end dates are validated against overlap rules.

sessions.title: Sessions management
sessions.subtitle: Manage operational exam sessions with cycle, location, and lifecycle controls.
sessions.description: Session records are linked to cycles and buildings, include stored and derived status, and enforce strict transition rules.
sessions.deleteHelp: Deactivate keeps the session for audit/history and hides it from active workflows. Permanent delete removes it completely and is available only when no assignments, waiting-list entries, or evaluations exist.

form.subtitle: Session timing must stay inside the selected cycle range and avoid building overlaps.
form.dateRangeInvalid: Start date and time must be earlier than end date and time.

assignmentPlaceholder.title: Assignments readiness
assignmentPlaceholder.body: After saving the session, start from Assignments for manual or auto distribution, then use Waiting List and Swaps as needed. Attendance comes after locking, and Evaluations follow the session run.

statusErrors.invalid_session_status_transition: This status transition is not allowed from the current session state.

statusErrors.session_lock_validation_failed: Session lock validation failed. Resolve assignment coverage and policy issues before locking.

workspace.exportBody: Data exports require setting a Session ID filter.
workspace.noExport: No export options are available for the current filters.

testGuide.title: Real testing guide
testGuide.subtitle: Bilingual operational guide built from executed workflows, service-layer rules, and live production checks.
testGuide.intro: This page documents what the current system actually does. Public production checks were executed against the live Vercel deployment, while authenticated workflows were executed against the seeded local runtime because production login is still blocked by a deployment-side auth/database issue.

live.title: Live production check
live.body: These checks were executed directly against the public deployment on March 21, 2026.
items: Production / redirects to /login and /login renders successfully.
Production /dashboard is protected and redirects to /login without a session.

local.title: Executed seeded flows
local.body: These authenticated workflows were executed against the seeded local runtime to verify real behavior end to end.
items: Executed seeded assignment, waiting-list, swap, attendance, evaluation, promotion, reporting, dashboard, and notification verifiers locally.
Seed data includes multiple active buildings, multi-building sessions, active/cancelled assignments, attendance, evaluations, blocks, preferences, and templates.
Authenticated route verification used the seeded admin account and DB-backed sessions.

rules.title: Service-layer guardrails
rules.body: The guide below is tied to backend contracts and validation, not only to visible UI labels.
items: Assignments run only before execution; attendance starts after LOCKED; evaluations open after session start.
Session locking depends on pre-lock validation; promotions depend on settings thresholds.
Notification delivery respects per-user preferences and safe fallback behavior.

access.title: Access & orientation
access.description: Use these sections first to confirm environment readiness, login behavior, and the dashboard entry points.

setup.title: Core data setup
setup.description: These flows define the hierarchy and master data that power operational sessions and assignments.

operations.title: Operational workflows
operations.description: These sections cover the real staffing lifecycle from assignment planning through attendance, evaluation, promotion, and blocking.

oversight.title: Oversight & output
oversight.description: Use these areas to verify exported outputs, summaries, and multi-channel notifications.

quality.title: Quality control
quality.description: Keep these reference sections close during test execution to speed up diagnosis and keep runs repeatable.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.summary: Prepare seed data, access, and runtime before protected testing begins.
gettingStarted.purpose: Set up a reliable baseline before testing protected flows.
steps: Run the Prisma seed so roles, settings, app users, buildings, sessions, assignments, waiting-list entries, evaluations, blocks, and templates exist.
Use a seeded admin or coordinator account for protected testing.
Confirm that target sessions are linked to active buildings before testing assignments or reports.
gettingStarted.expected: Accounts, locations, sessions, role definitions, and notification templates are available before operational testing starts.
notes: The live production deployment is reachable, but protected live testing is blocked by auth_service_unavailable at login.
Authenticated workflow execution in this guide was validated against the seeded local runtime.

login.title: Login
login.summary: Validate the database-backed session flow and the current failure path.
login.purpose: Confirm authentication, redirect behavior, and readable failure handling.
steps: Open /login and submit a seeded email and password.
Confirm that valid credentials create an examops_session cookie and redirect to /dashboard.
Retry with invalid credentials or with an unavailable auth backend to confirm the error state.
login.expected: Valid credentials redirect into the protected shell, while invalid or unavailable auth returns a localized error message.
notes: On the live production URL, POST /api/auth/login currently redirects to /login?error=auth_service_unavailable.
Local verifiers authenticated successfully with admin@examops.local and redirectTo=/dashboard.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.summary: Review the overview cards, metrics loading, and quick links.
dashboard.purpose: Confirm that the dashboard resolves protected metrics and gives fast access to reports, sessions, and the test guide.
steps: Open /dashboard after sign-in.
Verify the top metric cards and status-distribution sections.
Use the quick links to reports, sessions, and the test guide.
dashboard.expected: The dashboard loads sessions, assignments, and attendance metrics and renders localized EN/AR content.
notes: Local phase 8 step 7 verification passed for premium layout, theme rendering, localized views, and report links.
Unauthenticated production /dashboard redirects to /login as expected.

locations.title: Locations
locations.summary: Manage the hierarchy that sessions, reports, and room coverage depend on.
locations.purpose: Build a valid governorate-to-room structure before session and assignment testing.
steps: Create or import governorates, universities, buildings, floors, and rooms.
Keep codes and names unique within the correct parent scope.
For rooms, set supported exam types and a valid capacity range.
locations.expected: Search works across Arabic/English names and code, and inactive records remain hidden unless explicitly requested.
notes: Rooms require at least one supported exam type.
capacityMax must be greater than or equal to capacityMin, and child records cannot attach to missing or inactive required parents.

proctors.title: Proctors
proctors.summary: Maintain the staffing pool used by assignments, waiting list, evaluations, and promotion.
proctors.purpose: Keep the proctor master list clean, unique, and operationally reusable.
steps: Create a proctor with normalized phone, source, and optional governorate.
Keep phone, email, and national ID unique across the user table.
Use active status to control whether the proctor remains selectable in operations.
proctors.expected: Proctors load with aggregate counts for assignments, waiting-list entries, evaluations, and blocks.
notes: Phone validation and normalization run before save.
Governorate links cannot point to inactive parents, and duplicate phone/email/national ID is rejected.

cyclesSessions.title: Cycles & Sessions
cyclesSessions.summary: Create the operational calendar and the real execution windows.
cyclesSessions.purpose: Define valid cycles and sessions before assignment and attendance work begins.
steps: Create a cycle with a localized name and a valid start/end date range.
Create sessions with explicit timezone-aware startDateTime and endDateTime plus at least one linked building.
Advance session status only when timing and lock-validation rules are satisfied.
cyclesSessions.expected: Sessions stay within the cycle range, active cycles do not overlap, and active sessions do not overlap by time inside the same building.
notes: Sessions cannot span multiple calendar days.
Locking a session triggers pre-lock validation from the assignments service before status changes to LOCKED.

assignments.title: Assignments
assignments.summary: Place proctors into scoped roles manually or through auto-assignment.
assignments.purpose: Create valid staffing placements without duplicates, overlap conflicts, or scope violations.
steps: For manual assignment, submit sessionId, userId, buildingId, roleDefinitionId, and any required floor/room placement.
For auto assignment, choose the target session and optionally limit roles or candidate users.
Re-run late-import reranking when new candidates are added after the first plan.
assignments.expected: Duplicate assignments, blocked users, invalid building links, scope mismatches, and overlap conflicts are rejected.
notes: Manual creation is allowed only before session execution starts.
Auto assignment uses active linked buildings, active role definitions, overlap filtering, and settings-driven minimum rating.

waitingList.title: Waiting List
waitingList.summary: Hold backup candidates and promote them into real placements when needed.
waitingList.purpose: Manage a ranked reserve pool without creating duplicate assignments.
steps: Add a user to the waiting list with optional building and role preferences.
Review priority order before promotion.
Promote an entry into an assignment or remove it with a reason.
waitingList.expected: A user can have only one waiting-list entry per session, and users with existing assignments are rejected.
notes: Waiting entries rerank by average rating, then total sessions, then oldest createdAt.
Promotion requires a resolved building and role and creates a manual assignment.

swap.title: Swap
swap.summary: Move an existing placement to a different user or assignment without rebuilding the session.
swap.purpose: Use safe replacement and exchange workflows when staffing changes after planning.
steps: Use direct assignment swap to exchange two assignments in the same session.
Use waiting-list replacement to replace one assignee with a waiting candidate.
Use manual replacement to inject a different eligible user directly.
swap.expected: Session mismatch, duplicate assignment, overlap conflict, blocked replacement user, and no-op swaps are rejected.
notes: Direct swaps require matching roles unless manualOverride is enabled.
Replacement flows can demote the current assignee back to WAITING with swap_demotion source.

attendance.title: Attendance
attendance.summary: Record who appeared and trigger controlled replacements when needed.
attendance.purpose: Track operational attendance after locking and promote replacements only through allowed states.
steps: Update attendance to CONFIRMED, ABSENT, or DECLINED after the session is locked.
Use replacementWaitingListId only when the status is ABSENT or DECLINED.
Review replacement suggestions before promoting a waiting candidate.
attendance.expected: Attendance creates or updates one record per assignment, stores check-in time for CONFIRMED, and can promote a replacement safely.
notes: Local attendance verification passed for update, replacement suggestions, absent replacement, rollback safety, and activity logs.
When a replacement is promoted, the original assignment is cancelled after the new assignment is created.

evaluation.title: Evaluation
evaluation.summary: Score proctor performance after session execution begins.
evaluation.purpose: Capture validated performance scores that also update the subject user's average rating.
steps: After the session starts, submit sessionId, assignmentId, userId, rating, and notes.
Use allowUpdate only when the same evaluator intentionally edits an existing evaluation.
Re-check the subject user's rating after create or update.
evaluation.expected: Duplicate evaluation, invalid rating, self-evaluation, cancelled-assignment evaluation, and assignment/session/user mismatch are rejected.
notes: Ratings allow up to two decimal places.
Every create or update recalculates the subject user's averageRating from stored evaluations.

promotion.title: Promotion
promotion.summary: Rank upgrade candidates from real historical performance.
promotion.purpose: Review promotion suggestions based on rating, attendance, and completed-session history instead of manual guesswork.
steps: Open promotion suggestions and optionally limit the number of returned rows.
Review rank, score, average rating, attendance ratio, and completed-session totals.
Confirm that blocked users are excluded from the candidate list.
promotion.expected: Only users that meet minimum settings thresholds for rating, sessions, and attendance ratio are returned.
notes: Suggestion score is weighted 50% rating, 30% attendance, and 20% completed sessions.
Local promotion verification passed for ranking correctness, threshold filtering, and blocked-user exclusion.

blocks.title: Blocks
blocks.summary: Prevent risky users from re-entering assignment workflows until the restriction is lifted or expires.
blocks.purpose: Use permanent or temporary blocks to stop assignment, waiting-list, and replacement eligibility when needed.
steps: Create a PERMANENT or TEMPORARY block for the target user.
For TEMPORARY, provide a future endsAt value.
Use unblock to lift the current restriction when operationally appropriate.
blocks.expected: Active blocked users are rejected by assignment, waiting-list, and replacement flows.
notes: Permanent blocks cannot include an end date.

Local debugging confirmed that active temporary blocks reject manual assignment and eligibility returns after expiry; the legacy block verifier itself was brittle because the temporary window was too short.

reportsExport.title: Reports & Export
reportsExport.summary: Review summary outputs and download session-scoped files.
reportsExport.purpose: Verify reporting totals, breakdowns, filters, and exports from real APIs.
steps: Open assignments, attendance, or evaluations reports.
Apply optional sessionId, cycleId, or locationId filters.
Use export links when a sessionId is present.
reportsExport.expected: Metrics cards and breakdowns render from live APIs, and exports download as CSV, Excel, or PDF for the selected session.
notes: Filter mismatches are rejected, such as a sessionId paired with a cycle or location that does not match the session.
Local reporting verification passed for export structure, empty-data handling, no-duplicate rows, aggregations, and report logging.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.summary: Deliver assignment, attendance, waiting-list, and block updates across multiple channels.
notifications.purpose: Verify trigger execution, in-app delivery, channel preferences, and safe fallback behavior.
steps: Maintain active bilingual email templates for supported event types.
Review the in-app inbox, read/read-all actions, and user channel preferences.
Test trigger-driven delivery and fallback behavior with safe provider settings.
notifications.expected: assignment_created, assignment_swapped, attendance_marked, waiting_list_promoted, and user_block_status_changed prepare localized payloads and respect user preferences.
notes: Local notification verification passed for triggers, in-app inbox, SMS fallback, preference enforcement, and no-crash behavior when channels are disabled.
Email, WhatsApp, and SMS gracefully skip or fail-safe when required config is missing.

commonErrors.title: Common Errors
commonErrors.summary: Map frequent failures back to the real backend rule quickly.
commonErrors.purpose: Speed up diagnosis by checking the typed error code before changing data blindly.
steps: Check the returned error code, message, and linked entity IDs.
Reproduce one workflow at a time with clean fixture data.
Clear duplicate data or invalid links before re-testing.
commonErrors.expected: Most operational failures return a typed code such as user_blocked, duplicate_assignment, duplicate_waiting_list_entry, invalid_date_range, overlapping_session, or duplicate_evaluation.
notes: Live production currently shows auth_service_unavailable at login, which blocks protected testing there until the deployment database/auth runtime is fixed.
Local seeded flows remain the reliable reference for protected workflow behavior.

bestPractices.title: Best Practices
bestPractices.summary: Keep testing deterministic, realistic, and easy to audit.
bestPractices.purpose: Run release validation in a way that reflects production behavior without introducing flaky results.
steps: Seed before testing and use explicit timezone-aware datetimes for sessions.
Link realistic sessions to multiple active buildings before testing assignment coverage and exports.
Lock sessions only after pre-lock issues are cleared, and use waiting list or swap flows instead of forcing duplicate manual assignments.
bestPractices.expected: Tests stay repeatable, logs remain readable, and reports or exports reflect realistic operational data.
notes: Keep notification preferences intentional and verify exports with session-scoped filters.
Leave enough time buffer when validating temporary block expiry or other time-based transitions.

footer.title: Execution note
footer.body: Switch the shell locale if you want the page chrome in Arabic or English. Each section intentionally keeps both EN and AR workflow panels visible for side-by-side tester review.
